title.zh: 玻璃中的時間膠囊：激光寫入技術讓數字資料存儲超過一萬年
title.en: Glass as the Ultimate Time Capsule: Laser Writing Could Store Our Digital Legacy for 10,000 Years

summary.zh:
【總結】Silica首次實現了生產級玻璃存儲技術，讓數字資料在玻璃中「永生」——數據可存10,000年以上，寫入速度達65.9 Mbit/s，密度高達0.678 Gbit/mm³。研究突破在於同步解決了「寫入效率、資料密度、系統可靠性」三大瓶頸：透過雙軸體（birefringent/phase）寫入技術，結合機器學習解碼，將散射和干擾問題降到最低。這不僅是材料科學的進步，更為人類數位文明的長期保存提供終極方案。

【研究問題】當前數位存儲介質（如硬碟、雲端）壽命僅數年，每年需耗費大量資源進行數據遷移。過去的玻璃存儲技術雖理論可行，卻因寫入速度慢、能量效率低、穩定性差，始終無法投入實用。本研究直指數位時代的存儲危機：我們在生成數據的同時，卻沒有足夠耐用的「數位膠囊」保存這些珍貴記憶。

【研究方法】研究團隊設計兩種光學「微像素」寫入技術：1. 極化軸體（birefringent）採用「類單脈衝」法，用兩束脈衝快速形成納米孔；2. 相位軸體（phase）以單脈衝調節折射率，實現多比特存儲。結合多光束並行寫入（4束光同步操作）與機器學習解碼（CNN神經網路），成功解決散射干擾問題。寫入過程透過實時光譜監測，自動調整雷射能量，確保每一個微像素的精準度。

【結果】相位軸體實現0.678 Gbit/mm³密度，寫入效率達8.85 nJ/位元，多光束系統寫入速度突破65.9 Mbit/s。加速老化實驗證實，數據在室溫下可穩定保存超過10,000年。這項技術不僅讓玻璃存儲從實驗室邁向產業化，更可能徹底改變雲端存儲的未來——讓數位時代的珍貴記憶真正「永恆流傳」。
summary.en:
**Summary:** The Silica system marks a quantum leap in archival storage, transforming glass into a durable, high-capacity medium for millennia-long data preservation. By pioneering two novel laser-writing regimes—birefringent and phase voxels—alongside machine learning decoding, the team overcomes the critical limitations of existing storage solutions. This isn't just an improvement; it's a paradigm shift that could safeguard humanity's digital legacy for generations.

**Problem:** With data generation doubling every three years, current archival systems—relying on media that degrade within years—demand costly, energy-intensive migrations. Previous glass-based storage efforts failed to balance throughput, energy efficiency, and reliability. The urgency is clear: without a robust, scalable solution, our digital heritage risks becoming a fleeting echo of the past.

**Approach:** Researchers engineered a dual-path voxel-writing system: birefringent voxels using "pseudo-single-pulse" techniques for rapid nanovoid formation, and phase voxels with single-pulse energy modulation to minimize scattering. Multibeam writing (four parallel beams) boosted throughput to 65.9 Mbit/s, while convolutional neural networks decoded noisy signals, overcoming cross-voxel interference. Real-time emission monitoring ensured precise, stable writing.

**Results:** Silica achieved 0.678 Gbit/mm³ density (2.02 TB per 120mm glass platter) with 10,000+ year stability at room temperature. Its write efficiency of 8.85 nJ per bit and scalable throughput position it as a viable cloud-scale archival solution. This work bridges material science and AI, offering a practical answer to humanity's urgent need for digital immortality.

keyArticle.title: Laser writing in glass for dense, fast and efficient archival data storage
